steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Reading comprehension — logical completion of a conclusion. Key facts: Ancestral Puebloans dispersed in the late 1200s; Rio Grande Valley turkeys share genetic markers with Mesa Verde turkeys only after 1280; Mesa Verde villages had turkey farming.

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: Mesa Verde turkey genes appearing in Rio Grande turkeys right after the late-1200s dispersal best supports the inference that Mesa Verde people moved to the Rio Grande Valley, bringing their farmed turkeys (and farming practices) with them.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — conditions of the terrains in the Rio Grande Valley and Mesa Verde had greater similarities in the past than they do today.: passage gives no evidence about historical terrain comparison; introduces a claim the data don't support. ✗
  • Choice C — Indigenous peoples living in the Rio Grande Valley primarily planted crops and did not cultivate turkeys before 1280.: shared markers appearing after 1280 doesn't prove the absence of earlier turkey cultivation there — it only shows Mesa Verde lineage arrived then. Overreach. ✗
  • Choice D — the Ancestral Puebloans of Mesa Verde likely adopted the farming practices of Indigenous peoples living in other regions.: reverses the direction of influence shown by the genetic evidence (Mesa Verde turkeys ended up in the Rio Grande, not the other way around). ✗
That is the correct answer.
